lifecycleFinding: In force with phased application under the 2024 text. Chapters I and II applied from 2 February 2025 and further listed provisions from 2 August 2025. lifecycleFinding: AI RMF 1.0 remains NIST's current published voluntary core framework while a revision is in progress. Profiles and the Playbook are separate resources and do not silently replace the core or establish legal compliance.

authoritativeLanguage.note: NIST AI 100-1 is an original English federal technical publication. It is a voluntary risk-management framework, not legislation or an independently sufficient statement of legal compliance.

englishAvailability.note: All 135 hierarchical nodes preserve the NIST AI RMF 1.0 publication, including four functions, 19 categories, and 72 subcategories. Its voluntary status and third-party-material boundary remain explicit.

localCoverage.statement: The complete NIST AI RMF 1.0 publication is stored in 135 hierarchical nodes, including four functions, 19 categories, and all 72 subcategories. The corpus remains voluntary and preserves a separate third-party-material license boundary.

rightsBoundary.note: U.S. Government-authored NIST material is subject to 17 U.S.C. § 105 and NIST's worldwide reuse terms. Cited or adapted ISO/OECD and other third-party material remains separately protected and excluded from the project license.

sources.label: NIST
NIST AI 100-1 — controlling complete official edition
NIST AI Risk Management Framework hub — current framework and revision status

caveats: The complete NIST AI 100-1 publication is stored in 135 hierarchical text nodes. Identified ISO/OECD material remains excluded from the project license and any blanket public-domain claim.
AI RMF 1.0 is voluntary, non-binding, and law- and regulation-agnostic; a mapped outcome does not itself establish compliance with any legal regime.
NIST is revising the framework, but the revision process, Playbook, and Generative AI Profile do not silently replace the published 1.0 core corpus.
Identified ISO- and OECD-derived material incorporated by NIST is excluded from the project's license and from any blanket public-domain claim.
